title: lupinus lyallii subsp. alcis-temporis
intro: Lupinus lyallii subsp. alcis-temporis, commonly known as a rare alpine lupine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family, native to New Zealand's South Island. The medicinal properties of Lupinus lyallii subsp. alcis-temporis include its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Lupinus lyallii subsp. alcis-temporis can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
